Trailer Park Shepherd's Pie

Trailer Park Shepherd's Pie

An Original Recipe by Not Quite Nigella. Serves 4 generously.

  • 1 small onion, chopped

  • 14 tablespoons or 210 grams or 1 and 3/4 sticks butter

  • 550g beef mince

  • 410g tin of chopped tomatoes or 2x 8 oz cans tomato sauce

  • 1.5 cups frozen mixed vegetables

  • 40 ounce / 1.2 litre beer

  • salt and pepper

  • Tabasco sauce to taste

  • 8-10 medium red-skinned potatoes

  • 1.5 cups milk

  • 1/2 cup sour cream

  • 1 1/4 cups grated mozzarella cheese

  • 1 garlic clove, finely diced

  • 1 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese

  • 1/4 cup breadcrumbs

  • 2 cups biscuit mix

  • Nonstick cooking spray

Beef layer

Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the butter and chopped onion and saute until the onion is translucent. Add the beef mince and brown it, breaking it apart with a wooden spoon as it cooks. Stir in the chopped tomatoes, frozen mixed vegetables, and beer. Season with salt, pepper, and Tabasco to taste. Simmer for 15 minutes until thickened.

Potato layer

Peel and chop the potatoes, then boil in salted water until tender (about 15 minutes). Drain and mash with the milk, sour cream, mozzarella, garlic, and half of the parmesan. Season to taste.

Biscuit layer

Combine the biscuit mix with enough water to form a soft dough. Roll out and cut into rounds.

To assemble

Preheat the oven to 200C / 400F. Spray a large baking dish with nonstick spray. Spread the beef mixture across the bottom, then top with the mashed potatoes. Sprinkle with the breadcrumbs and remaining parmesan. Arrange the biscuit rounds on top. Bake for 20-25 minutes until the biscuits are golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
